# Python API reference (`lake.*`)

Public methods on each `lake.<namespace>` accessor, generated by introspecting the namespace classes. Open a lake with `Lake.open(uri)` (or `Lake.init(uri)`), then reach a namespace as `lake.<namespace>`.

## `lake.align` — `LakeAlign`

Convenience namespace exposed as ``lake.align``.

- `align.create_view(name: 'str', *, source: 'str' = 'observations', clock: 'str' = 'timestamp_ns', rate_hz: 'float', streams: 'Sequence[str]', tolerance_ms: 'float | None' = None, interpolation: 'Mapping[str, str] | str | None' = None, latency_ns: 'Mapping[str, int] | None' = None, run_id: 'str | None' = None, start_time_ns: 'int | None' = None, end_time_ns: 'int | None' = None, created_by: 'str' = 'lancedb-robotics', materialize: 'bool' = True, batch_size: 'int' = 8192) -> 'AlignmentView'` — Resample named observation streams onto one query clock and record lineage.
- `align.window(*, name: 'str', rate_hz: 'float', streams: 'Sequence[str]', run_id: 'str | None' = None, start_time_ns: 'int | None' = None, end_time_ns: 'int | None' = None, clock: 'str' = 'timestamp_ns', tolerance_ms: 'float | None' = None, interpolation: 'Mapping[str, str] | str | None' = None, latency_ns: 'Mapping[str, int] | None' = None, batch_size: 'int' = 8192) -> 'AlignmentView'` — Compute an aligned view without writing lineage rows.

## `lake.curate` — `LakeCurate`

Facade exposed as ``lake.curate``.

- `curate.compare(left: str, right: str, *, by: collections.abc.Sequence[str] = (), metrics: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | None = None, plugins: collections.abc.Sequence[lancedb_robotics.comparison_plugins.ComparisonMetricPlugin | str] | None = None, batch_size: int | None = None, preview_limit: int | None = None, created_by: str = 'lancedb-robotics') -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationComparison` — Compare two dataset snapshots by membership, curation metrics, and lineage.
- `curate.comparison(id_or_name: str) -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationComparison` — Reload a persisted comparison by ``comparison_id`` or snapshot-pair alias.
- `curate.comparison_membership(id_or_name: str, *, field: str | None = None, offset: int = 0, limit: int | None = None, page_token: str | None = None) -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationComparisonMembershipPage` — Page one membership-delta id list for a persisted comparison (0094).
- `curate.comparison_staleness(id_or_name: str) -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationComparisonStaleness` — Report whether a persisted comparison's source tables have advanced (0094).
- `curate.compile_row_plan(*, view_name: str, target_grain: str = 'observation', source_snapshot_name: str | None = None, include_decisions: collections.abc.Sequence[str] = ('include', 'promote'), excluding_decisions: collections.abc.Sequence[str] = ('exclude', 'defer', 'needs-review', 'reject'), freeze: bool = False, created_by: str = 'lancedb-robotics') -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationCompiledRowPlan` — Compile a saved view's latest membership decisions into a row plan.
- `curate.diff_snapshots(left: str, right: str, *, created_by: str = 'lancedb-robotics') -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationComparison` — Return and record the reproducible selected-ID diff between two snapshots.
- `curate.eval_metric_staleness(id_or_run: str) -> lancedb_robotics.curate.EvalMetricStaleness` — Report whether an eval metric's source tables have advanced (0095).
- `curate.feedback_from_eval(snapshot_name: str, *, metrics: collections.abc.Iterable[dict[str, Any]] | dict[str, Any], training_run_id: str = '', model_version: str = '', evaluation_run_id: str = '', regression_threshold: float = 0.0, created_by: str = 'lancedb-robotics') -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationFeedbackReport` — Import evaluation metrics and link them to an exact curated snapshot.
- `curate.from_gaps(gaps: Any, *, source: lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationScope | lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationView | dict | str | collections.abc.Sequence[str] | None = None, limit_per_gap: int | None = None, exclude_scenario_ids: collections.abc.Sequence[str] = (), created_by: str = 'lancedb-robotics') -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationSelection` — Create a candidate selection from missing or underrepresented slices.
- `curate.index_predicates(*, include_view_chunks: bool = True, refresh: bool = False) -> tuple[dict[str, typing.Any], ...]` — Build scalar indexes for saved-view and membership-decision lookups.
- `curate.index_review_queue_predicates(*, refresh: bool = False) -> tuple[dict[str, typing.Any], ...]` — Build scalar indexes for review queue reopen, pagination, and import.
- `curate.list_comparisons(*, snapshot: str | None = None, left: str | None = None, right: str | None = None, metric: str | None = None, state: str | None = None, include_archived: bool = True, include_pruned: bool = True, since: datetime.datetime | None = None, until: datetime.datetime | None = None, created_by: str | None = None, limit: int | None = None) -> tuple[lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationComparisonEntry, ...]` — List persisted comparison reports from the catalog, newest first.
- `curate.list_eval_metrics(*, snapshot: str | None = None, evaluation_run: str | None = None, training_run: str | None = None, model_version: str | None = None, metric: str | None = None, slice_label: str | None = None, regressed_only: bool = False, state: str | None = None, latest_only: bool = False, since: datetime.datetime | None = None, until: datetime.datetime | None = None, limit: int | None = None) -> lancedb_robotics.curate.EvalMetricListing` — List imported eval metrics from the catalog, newest first (0095).
- `curate.materialization_report(snapshot_name: str, *, target_format: str, output_uri: str = '', mode: str = 'projection', copied_payload_bytes: int = 0, metadata_bytes_written: int = 0, planned_payload_bytes: int = 0, projection_transform_id: str = '', created_by: str = 'lancedb-robotics') -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationMaterializationReport` — Record byte/copy accounting for a logical snapshot boundary projection.
- `curate.next_candidates(*, from_regressions: Any, source: lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationScope | lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationView | dict | str | collections.abc.Sequence[str] | None = None, queue_name: str | None = None, view_name: str | None = None, snapshot_name: str | None = None, snapshot_tag: str | None = None, limit_per_regression: int = 50, create_queue: bool = True, embedding_column: str = 'embedding', assignee: str = '', status: str = 'open', route: str = 'auto', nprobes: int | None = None, refine_factor: int | None = None, preview_limit: int = 100, dry_run: bool = False, created_by: str = 'lancedb-robotics') -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationCandidateReport` — Turn eval regressions into the next review queue, saved view, or snapshot.
- `curate.plan_comparison(left: str, right: str, *, by: collections.abc.Sequence[str] = (), metrics: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | None = None, plugins: collections.abc.Sequence[lancedb_robotics.comparison_plugins.ComparisonMetricPlugin | str] | None = None, batch_size: int | None = None, local_row_budget: int | None = None) -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationComparisonPlan` — Plan a comparison before running it (backlog 0094).
- `curate.plan_next_candidates(*, from_regressions: Any, source: lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationScope | lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationView | dict | str | collections.abc.Sequence[str] | None = None, queue_name: str | None = None, view_name: str | None = None, snapshot_name: str | None = None, snapshot_tag: str | None = None, limit_per_regression: int = 50, create_queue: bool = True, embedding_column: str = 'embedding', route: str = 'auto', nprobes: int | None = None, refine_factor: int | None = None, preview_limit: int = 100) -> lancedb_robotics.curate.FeedbackCandidatePlan` — Explain regression-seeded candidate generation without writing anything (0096).
- `curate.predicate_index_status(*, include_view_chunks: bool = True) -> tuple[dict[str, typing.Any], ...]` — Describe scalar predicate-index availability for curation hot paths.
- `curate.preview_candidates(plan: lancedb_robotics.curate.FeedbackCandidatePlan, *, cursor: str | None = None, limit: int | None = None) -> lancedb_robotics.curate.FeedbackCandidatePreviewPage` — Page a plan's complete candidate selection deterministically (0096).
- `curate.promote_snapshot(snapshot_name: str, *, decision: str = 'promote', reason: str, reason_code: str = '', reviewer: str = '', evaluation_run_id: str = '', training_run_id: str = '', model_version: str = '', metrics: collections.abc.Iterable[dict[str, Any]] | dict[str, Any] | None = None, comparison_id: str = '', created_by: str = 'lancedb-robotics') -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationPromotionDecision` — Record a promotion or rejection decision for a candidate snapshot.
- `curate.prune_comparisons(*, retain_latest: int = 1, older_than: datetime.datetime | datetime.timedelta | None = None, dry_run: bool = False, created_by: str = 'lancedb-robotics') -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationComparisonRetentionReport` — Apply the comparison retention lifecycle (active -> archived -> pruned).
- `curate.prune_eval_metrics(*, retain_latest: int = 1, older_than: datetime.datetime | datetime.timedelta | None = None, dry_run: bool = False, created_by: str = 'lancedb-robotics') -> lancedb_robotics.curate.EvalMetricRetentionReport` — Apply the eval-metric retention lifecycle (active -> superseded -> pruned).
- `curate.queue(name_or_id: str) -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationReviewQueue` — Reopen the latest review queue by queue name or queue id.
- `curate.resolve_membership(*, view_name: str | None = None, view_id: str | None = None, target_grain: str | None = 'scenario', target_ids: collections.abc.Sequence[str] = (), scenario_ids: collections.abc.Sequence[str] = (), as_of: datetime.datetime | str | None = None, transform_id: str | None = None, snapshot_name: str | None = None, superseded_policy: str = 'latest') -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationDecisionResolution` — Resolve latest membership decisions as of a time, transform, or snapshot.
- `curate.review_queue_predicate_index_status() -> tuple[dict[str, typing.Any], ...]` — Describe scalar predicate-index availability for review queue lookups.
- `curate.sync_eval_metric_catalog(*, build_indexes: bool = True, created_by: str = 'lancedb-robotics') -> lancedb_robotics.curate.EvalMetricCatalogSyncReport` — Rebuild ``eval_metric_catalog`` from ``model_outputs`` (0095).
- `curate.trace_membership(snapshot_name: str, scenario_id: str, *, superseded_policy: str = 'history') -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationMembershipTrace` — Explain why a scenario was included in or excluded from a snapshot.
- `curate.view(name: str) -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationSelection` — Reopen the latest saved curation view by name.
- `curate.workbench(*, scope: lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationScope | lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationView | dict | str | collections.abc.Sequence[str] | None = None, apply_decisions: bool = False) -> lancedb_robotics.curate.CurationSelection` — Open a composable curation workbench over matching scenarios.

## `lake.distributions` — `LakeDistributions`

Facade exposed as ``lake.distributions``.

- `distributions.compact_reports(*, older_than: datetime.datetime | datetime.timedelta | None = None, retain_latest_per_name: int = 1, kinds: collections.abc.Sequence[str] = ('report', 'comparison'), dry_run: bool = False, created_by: str = 'lancedb-robotics') -> lancedb_robotics.distributions.DistributionRetentionReport` — Compact expired report/comparison bodies while keeping audit metadata.
- `distributions.compare(*, observed: lancedb_robotics.distributions.DistributionReport | collections.abc.Mapping[str, Any], target: lancedb_robotics.distributions.DistributionReport | collections.abc.Mapping[str, Any], overrepresented_threshold: float = 0.05, created_by: str = 'lancedb-robotics') -> lancedb_robotics.distributions.DistributionComparison` — Compare an observed report against a target report and emit gaps.
- `distributions.define(*, name: str, dimensions: collections.abc.Sequence[str], min_count_per_slice: int = 0, slice_min_counts: collections.abc.Mapping[str, int] | None = None, weights: collections.abc.Mapping[str, float] | None = None, scope: Any = None, created_by: str = 'lancedb-robotics') -> lancedb_robotics.distributions.DistributionSpec` — Define the dimensions and minimum counts for a distribution report.
- `distributions.get_comparison(comparison_id: str) -> dict[str, typing.Any]` — Fetch a persisted distribution comparison body by id.
- `distributions.get_report(report_id: str) -> lancedb_robotics.distributions.DistributionReport` — Fetch a persisted report body by id.
- `distributions.latest_report(*, name: str | None = None, spec_id: str | None = None, source_kind: str | None = None, source_id: str | None = None, source_name: str | None = None, dataset_id: str | None = None, view_id: str | None = None, created_by: str | None = None) -> lancedb_robotics.distributions.DistributionReport` — Fetch the newest persisted report matching a name/spec/source filter.
- `distributions.list_catalog(*, kind: str | None = None, name: str | None = None, spec_id: str | None = None, report_id: str | None = None, comparison_id: str | None = None, source_kind: str | None = None, source_id: str | None = None, source_name: str | None = None, dataset_id: str | None = None, view_id: str | None = None, created_by: str | None = None, since: datetime.datetime | None = None, until: datetime.datetime | None = None, include_compacted: bool = True, limit: int | None = None) -> tuple[lancedb_robotics.distributions.DistributionCatalogEntry, ...]` — List persisted distribution artifacts from the catalog table.
- `distributions.list_reports(*, name: str | None = None, spec_id: str | None = None, source_kind: str | None = None, source_id: str | None = None, source_name: str | None = None, dataset_id: str | None = None, view_id: str | None = None, created_by: str | None = None, since: datetime.datetime | None = None, until: datetime.datetime | None = None, include_compacted: bool = True, limit: int | None = None) -> tuple[lancedb_robotics.distributions.DistributionCatalogEntry, ...]` — List persisted distribution reports without scanning transform JSON.
- `distributions.measure(spec: lancedb_robotics.distributions.DistributionSpec | collections.abc.Mapping[str, Any], *, source: Any = None, name: str | None = None, created_by: str = 'lancedb-robotics', execution: lancedb_robotics.distributions.DistributionExecutionOptions | collections.abc.Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, batch_size: int | None = None, max_slice_count: int | None = None, overflow: str | None = None, rare_slice_min_count: int | None = None, top_k_overflow: int | None = None, max_scenario_ids_per_slice: int | None = None, slice_bins: collections.abc.Mapping[str, str] | None = None) -> lancedb_robotics.distributions.DistributionReport` — Measure counts, percentages, quality, labels, and duplicate pressure.

## `lake.embeddings` — `LakeEmbeddings`

SDK surface for pluggable embedding creation (``lake.embeddings``).

- `embeddings.decoders() -> 'dict[str, str]'` — Registered decode steps mapped to their output kind.
- `embeddings.embed(spec: 'EmbeddingSpec', *, created_by: 'str' = 'lancedb-robotics', checkpoint_file: 'str | None' = None) -> 'ObservationEmbeddingReport'` — Run an :class:`EmbeddingSpec` against this lake.
- `embeddings.providers() -> 'dict[str, dict]'` — Registered providers with modality and (probe-only) availability.
- `embeddings.register(name: 'str', factory: 'Callable[..., EmbeddingProvider]', *, modality: 'str', requires_extra: 'bool' = False, extra_modules: 'Sequence[str]' = ()) -> 'None'` — Register a provider at runtime (in-process -- the prototype path).
- `embeddings.register_decoder(name: 'str', fn: 'Callable[..., object]', *, output: 'str', aux_columns: 'Sequence[str]' = ()) -> 'None'` — Register a decode step (raw input value + row aux -> model input).

## `lake.episodes` — `LakeEpisodes`

Convenience namespace exposed as ``lake.episodes``.

- `episodes.clear(transform_id: 'str', *, created_by: 'str' = 'lancedb-robotics') -> 'EpisodeLifecycleReport'` — Remove a derivation's rows and safely unset its current frame owners.
- `episodes.dry_run(recipe: 'Mapping[str, Any] | str', *, overlap_policy: 'str' = 'error') -> 'EpisodeDerivationDryRun | EpisodeMiningReport'` — Plan a recorded or ad hoc derivation recipe without writing tables.
- `episodes.from_intervals(intervals: 'Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]]', *, time_unit: 'str' = 'ns', allow_clipped: 'bool' = False, allow_empty: 'bool' = False, source_uri: 'str | None' = None, source_sha256: 'str | None' = None, overlap_policy: 'str' = 'error', created_by: 'str' = 'lancedb-robotics') -> 'EpisodeBuildReport'`
- `episodes.from_markers(*, start_event_types: 'Sequence[str] | None' = None, stop_event_types: 'Sequence[str] | None' = None, outcome_by_event_type: 'Mapping[str, str] | None' = None, overlap_policy: 'str' = 'error', created_by: 'str' = 'lancedb-robotics') -> 'EpisodeBuildReport'`
- `episodes.from_predicate(*, where: 'str | None' = None, source_table: 'str | None' = None, source_where: 'str | None' = None, before_ns: 'int' = 0, after_ns: 'int' = 0, merge_gap_ns: 'int | None' = None, min_duration_ns: 'int | None' = None, max_duration_ns: 'int | None' = None, topics: 'Sequence[str] | None' = None, modalities: 'Sequence[str] | None' = None, dry_run: 'bool' = False, overlap_policy: 'str' = 'error', created_by: 'str' = 'lancedb-robotics') -> 'EpisodeMiningReport'`
- `episodes.from_query(*, event_type: 'str', before_ns: 'int', after_ns: 'int', overlap_policy: 'str' = 'error', created_by: 'str' = 'lancedb-robotics') -> 'EpisodeBuildReport'`
- `episodes.from_scenarios(*, scenario_ids: 'Sequence[str] | None' = None, snapshot_name: 'str | None' = None, outcome: 'str | None' = None, outcome_by_coverage_tag: 'Mapping[str, str] | None' = None, outcome_by_summary: 'Mapping[str, str] | None' = None, task_id: 'str | None' = None, task_id_by_coverage_tag: 'Mapping[str, str] | None' = None, task_id_by_summary: 'Mapping[str, str] | None' = None, overlap_policy: 'str' = 'error', created_by: 'str' = 'lancedb-robotics') -> 'EpisodeBuildReport'`
- `episodes.get(episode_id: 'str') -> 'Episode'`
- `episodes.list_derivations() -> 'tuple[EpisodeDerivationSummary, ...]'` — Return the computed episode-derivation registry.
- `episodes.rebuild(transform_id: 'str', *, overlap_policy: 'str' = 'replace', created_by: 'str' = 'lancedb-robotics') -> 'EpisodeBuildReport | EpisodeMiningReport'` — Replay a recorded derivation recipe.
- `episodes.show_derivation(transform_id: 'str') -> 'EpisodeDerivationDetail'` — Return detailed recipe, output, and lifecycle state for a derivation.
- `episodes.supersede(old_transform_id: 'str', recipe: 'Mapping[str, Any] | str', *, created_by: 'str' = 'lancedb-robotics') -> 'EpisodeBuildReport | EpisodeMiningReport'` — Replace an older active derivation with a new recorded recipe.

## `lake.eval` — `LakeEval`

Convenience namespace exposed as ``lake.eval`` and ``lake.evaluation``.

- `eval.attach_external_refs(eval_run_id: 'str', refs: 'Mapping[str, Any]', *, replace: 'bool' = False) -> 'ExternalReferenceUpdate'` — Attach MLflow/W&B/etc. references to an existing evaluation manifest.
- `eval.expire(eval_run_id: 'str', *, force: 'bool' = False) -> 'dict[str, Any]'` — Delete an evaluation run (refused if protected unless ``force``).
- `eval.list(**kwargs: 'Any') -> 'ManifestPage'` — Deterministic paged evaluation listing with continuation tokens.
- `eval.metrics(**kwargs: 'Any') -> 'EvalMetricLookupResult'` — Metric-key lookup over the materialized eval-metric surface (or scan).
- `eval.query(**kwargs: 'Any') -> 'ManifestQueryResult'` — Bounded/indexed evaluation query with an execution plan (backlog 0100).
- `eval.record_run(snapshot: 'str | None' = None, **kwargs: 'Any') -> 'EvaluationRunManifest'` — Record an evaluation manifest for a model artifact and snapshot.
- `eval.retention_plan(*, older_than: 'timedelta | datetime | None' = None) -> 'ManifestRetentionPlan'` — Report which evaluation runs are protected vs safe to expire.
- `eval.runs(*, model_artifact_id: 'str | None' = None, dataset_id: 'str | None' = None, snapshot: 'str | None' = None) -> 'tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]'` — Return evaluation rows filtered by model artifact and/or snapshot.
- `eval.sync_metrics(*, build_indexes: 'bool' = True) -> 'EvalMetricSyncReport'` — Rebuild the materialized ``evaluation_run_metrics`` surface + indexes.

## `lake.lineage` — `LakeLineage`

Lineage graph recording, traversal, and checkpoint trace queries.

- `lineage.apply_retention_policy(policy_id: 'str', **kwargs)` — Expand an active policy into explicit artifact holds (idempotent, dry-run default).
- `lineage.artifact_id(kind: 'str', **identity: 'Any') -> 'str'` — Return the stable LanceDB Robotics artifact id for ``identity``.
- `lineage.audit(artifact: 'str | None' = None, *, kind: 'str | None' = None, refresh: 'bool' = True, check_sources: 'bool' = True, check_remote_sources: 'bool' = False, source_auth_ref: 'str | None' = None, source_storage_options: 'Mapping[str, Any] | None' = None, page_size: 'int | None' = None, page_token: 'str | None' = None, now: 'datetime | None' = None) -> 'LineageAuditReport'` — Audit lineage references, retention holds, and cleanup eligibility.
- `lineage.audit_findings(report_id_or_digest: 'str', **kwargs)` — Return a bounded page of findings from a persisted audit report (0112).
- `lineage.audit_reports(**kwargs)` — List persisted lineage audit reports newest-first (0112).
- `lineage.backfill_external_contexts(**kwargs)` — Index external run/job context from canonical rows into the catalog.
- `lineage.benchmark_rebuild_plan(artifact: 'str | None' = None, *, repeat: 'int' = 1, measure_memory: 'bool' = True, refresh: 'bool' = True, **plan_kwargs) -> 'dict[str, Any]'` — Benchmark rebuild planning: affected/action counts, time, peak memory.
- `lineage.check_hook_conformance(hook: 'Any', *, operation: 'str' = 'lineage-hook-conformance', params: 'Mapping[str, Any] | None' = None)` — Run the dependency-free hook plugin conformance harness.
- `lineage.clear_retention(artifact: 'str', *, kind: 'str | None' = None, refresh: 'bool' = True) -> 'LineageRetentionHold'` — Remove retention metadata from artifact handle(s).
- `lineage.compare_cypher_traversal(artifact: 'str', *, direction: 'str' = 'upstream', kind: 'str | None' = None, max_depth: 'int | None' = None)` — Compare SDK traversal against Cypher reachability for one handle (0099).
- `lineage.context(lineage_context: 'Any | None', *, inherit: 'bool' = True)` — Scope external lineage context across nested SDK operations.
- `lineage.current_context()` — Return the external lineage context active in this execution scope.
- `lineage.cypher(query_text: 'str', *, parameters: 'dict[str, Any] | None' = None, strategy: 'str | None' = None) -> 'list[dict[str, Any]]'` — Run a Cypher query over the lineage property graph (backlog 0099).
- `lineage.cypher_backend()` — Return the optional ``lance-graph`` Cypher backend (backlog 0099).
- `lineage.emission_divergence() -> 'LineageEmissionDivergence'` — Compare the materialized graph against a fresh full projection (0098 AC#4).
- `lineage.emit_datahub(*, refresh: 'bool' = True, target: 'str | None' = None, endpoint_url: 'str | None' = None, auth_ref: 'str | None' = None, headers: 'Mapping[str, str] | None' = None, client: 'Any | None' = None, adapter: 'str' = 'datahub', retry: 'bool' = False, created_by: 'str | None' = None)` — Deliver DataHub-style lineage edges and record delivery attempts.
- `lineage.emit_openlineage(*, refresh: 'bool' = True, producer: 'str | None' = None, target: 'str | None' = None, endpoint_url: 'str | None' = None, auth_ref: 'str | None' = None, headers: 'Mapping[str, str] | None' = None, client: 'Any | None' = None, adapter: 'str' = 'openlineage', retry: 'bool' = False, created_by: 'str | None' = None)` — Deliver OpenLineage events and record idempotent delivery attempts.
- `lineage.emit_transform(transform: 'Mapping[str, Any]', *, now: 'datetime | None' = None) -> 'EmittedLineage'` — Emit the lineage graph slice for one just-written ``transform_runs`` row.
- `lineage.emitted_transform_summary(transform_id: 'str') -> 'EmittedLineage | None'` — Read back the inline-emitted lineage summary for a transform id.
- `lineage.evidence_pack(artifact: 'str', *, kind: 'str | None' = None, checkpoint: 'bool' = False, where: 'str | None' = None, limit: 'int | None' = None, max_depth: 'int | None' = None, edge_types: 'Iterable[str]' = (), target_kinds: 'Iterable[str]' = (), refresh: 'bool' = True, output_dir: 'str | None' = None, materialize: 'bool' = False, include_payloads: 'bool' = False, include_attachments: 'bool' = False, include_video: 'bool' = False, redaction_policy: 'Any | None' = None)` — Build a source evidence pack for a checkpoint, model output, or artifact.
- `lineage.evidence_pack_events(**kwargs)` — Return evidence-pack audit events, optionally filtered.
- `lineage.evidence_retention_plan(**kwargs)` — Report which recorded packs are protected vs safe to expire.
- `lineage.expire_evidence_pack(digest: 'str', **kwargs)` — Delete a catalog entry, refused if protected unless force=True.
- `lineage.expire_external_context(context_id: 'str', **kwargs)` — Delete a recorded context row, refused if held unless force=True.
- `lineage.export_artifact_urns(*, backend: 'str' = 'openlineage', page_size: 'int | None' = None, page_token: 'str | None' = None, artifact_kind: 'str | None' = None, created_after: 'datetime | str | None' = None, created_before: 'datetime | str | None' = None, table_versions: 'Any | None' = None, refresh: 'bool' = True)` — Return a bounded page of the bulk artifact-URN catalog (backlog 0105).
- `lineage.export_datahub(*, refresh: 'bool' = True, dry_run: 'bool' = True)` — Project canonical lineage graph edges to DataHub-style lineage.
- `lineage.export_datahub_page(*, page_size: 'int | None' = None, page_token: 'str | None' = None, artifact_kind: 'str | None' = None, created_after: 'datetime | str | None' = None, created_before: 'datetime | str | None' = None, table_versions: 'Any | None' = None, refresh: 'bool' = True, dry_run: 'bool' = True)` — Return a bounded page of DataHub-style lineage edges (backlog 0105).
- `lineage.export_openlineage(*, refresh: 'bool' = True, dry_run: 'bool' = True, producer: 'str | None' = None)` — Project canonical lineage graph executions to OpenLineage events.
- `lineage.export_openlineage_page(*, page_size: 'int | None' = None, page_token: 'str | None' = None, execution_kind: 'str | None' = None, created_after: 'datetime | str | None' = None, created_before: 'datetime | str | None' = None, refresh: 'bool' = True, dry_run: 'bool' = True, producer: 'str | None' = None)` — Return a bounded page of OpenLineage RunEvents (backlog 0105).
- `lineage.export_rebuild_plan_dispatch(plan_id: 'str', **kwargs)` — Build a deterministic orchestrator handoff payload for a recorded plan.
- `lineage.export_retention_policy_state(**kwargs)` — Project policy + resolved-hold state out for external governance systems.
- `lineage.external_context_events(**kwargs)` — Return external-context audit events, optionally filtered.
- `lineage.external_context_retention_plan(**kwargs)` — Report which recorded contexts are held vs safe to expire.
- `lineage.external_urn(artifact_id: 'str', *, backend: 'str' = 'openlineage') -> 'str'` — Return a stable external URN for a canonical lineage artifact.
- `lineage.find_external_context(**kwargs)` — Resolve external run/job handles to canonical executions/artifacts (paged).
- `lineage.get_audit_report(report_id_or_digest: 'str')` — Reload a persisted lineage audit report by id or digest (0112).
- `lineage.get_external_context(context_id: 'str')` — Reload a single external-context catalog entry by id.
- `lineage.get_rebuild_plan(plan_id_or_digest: 'str')` — Reload a catalog entry and its stored plan payload by id or digest.
- `lineage.get_retention_policy(policy_id_or_digest: 'str')` — Reload a catalog entry and its stored policy definition by id or digest.
- `lineage.hook_adapters()` — Return known dependency-light lineage hook adapter specs.
- `lineage.impact(artifact: 'str', *, kind: 'str | None' = None, max_depth: 'int | None' = None, edge_types: 'Iterable[str]' = (), target_kinds: 'Iterable[str]' = (), created_after: 'datetime | str | None' = None, created_before: 'datetime | str | None' = None, table_versions: 'Mapping[str, int] | Iterable[str | tuple[str, int]]' = (), page_size: 'int | None' = None, page_token: 'str | None' = None) -> 'LineageGraph'` — Traverse downstream dependents for an artifact id or domain handle.
- `lineage.index_plan() -> 'dict[str, Any]'` — Return the required/present/missing lineage predicate index plan.
- `lineage.invalidate(artifact: 'str', *, kind: 'str | None' = None, reason: 'str', severity: 'str' = 'high', discovered_by: 'str | None' = None, actor: 'str | None' = None, replacement: 'str | None' = None, refresh: 'bool' = True) -> 'LineageInvalidation'` — Record a durable invalidation marker for an artifact or handle.
- `lineage.iter_artifact_urn_ndjson(*, backend: 'str' = 'openlineage', page_size: 'int' = 512, artifact_kind: 'str | None' = None, created_after: 'datetime | str | None' = None, created_before: 'datetime | str | None' = None, table_versions: 'Any | None' = None, refresh: 'bool' = True, include_summary: 'bool' = False)` — Stream bulk URN-catalog records one at a time (backlog 0105).
- `lineage.iter_audit_findings_ndjson(report_id_or_digest: 'str', **kwargs)` — Yield persisted audit findings as NDJSON records (0112).
- `lineage.iter_datahub_ndjson(*, page_size: 'int' = 512, artifact_kind: 'str | None' = None, created_after: 'datetime | str | None' = None, created_before: 'datetime | str | None' = None, table_versions: 'Any | None' = None, refresh: 'bool' = True, dry_run: 'bool' = True, include_summary: 'bool' = False)` — Stream DataHub-style edges one record at a time (backlog 0105).
- `lineage.iter_openlineage_ndjson(*, page_size: 'int' = 512, execution_kind: 'str | None' = None, created_after: 'datetime | str | None' = None, created_before: 'datetime | str | None' = None, refresh: 'bool' = True, dry_run: 'bool' = True, producer: 'str | None' = None, include_summary: 'bool' = False)` — Stream OpenLineage RunEvents one record at a time (backlog 0105).
- `lineage.lineage_delivery_attempts(*, backend: 'str | None' = None, target: 'str | None' = None, status: 'str | None' = None)` — Read persisted external lineage delivery attempts.
- `lineage.list_evidence_packs(**kwargs)` — List catalog entries newest-first, filtered and bounded by page_size.
- `lineage.load_evidence_pack(*, digest: 'str | None' = None, subject: 'str | None' = None)` — Reload a catalog entry and its manifest by digest or subject handle.
- `lineage.materialize_evidence_pack(pack, **kwargs)` — Materialize a pack's blobs to a local/object-store destination (chunked, resumable).
- `lineage.plan_evidence_materialization(pack, **kwargs)` — Build a bounded, chunked copy plan and enforce limits before any copy.
- `lineage.plan_refresh(*, incremental: 'bool' = True, force_full: 'bool' = False) -> 'LineageRefreshPlan'` — Return the refresh plan without mutating the graph (backlog 0097).
- `lineage.project_retention_state(sink, **kwargs)` — Build a governance projection and hand it to a caller-supplied sink.
- `lineage.rebuild_plan(artifact: 'str | None' = None, *, kind: 'str | None' = None, provider: 'str | None' = None, provider_version: 'str | None' = None, embedding_column: 'str | None' = None, reason: 'str | None' = None, severity: 'str' = 'high', discovered_by: 'str | None' = None, actor: 'str | None' = None, replacement: 'str | None' = None, record_invalidation: 'bool' = False, refresh: 'bool' = True, max_depth: 'int | None' = None, action_policy: 'ActionPolicy | Callable[[ActionContext], str | None] | None' = None, max_affected_artifacts: 'int | None' = None, max_actions: 'int | None' = None, require_indexes: 'bool' = False, page_size: 'int | None' = None, page_token: 'str | None' = None, summary: 'bool' = False) -> 'RebuildPlan'` — Return an ordered downstream rebuild plan for an invalidated handle.
- `lineage.rebuild_plan_events(**kwargs)` — Return rebuild-plan audit events, optionally filtered.
- `lineage.rebuild_plan_summary(artifact: 'str | None' = None, **kwargs) -> 'RebuildPlan'` — Return a summary-first rebuild plan (aggregates only, no action rows).
- `lineage.rebuild_plans(**kwargs)` — List recorded rebuild plans newest-first, filtered and bounded.
- `lineage.record_artifact(*, kind: 'str', artifact_id: 'str | None' = None, name: 'str | None' = None, table_name: 'str | None' = None, table_version: 'int | None' = None, table_tag: 'str | None' = None, row_grain: 'str | None' = None, row_ids: 'Iterable[str]' = (), source_uri: 'str | None' = None, source_id: 'str | None' = None, digest: 'str | None' = None, producer_execution_id: 'str | None' = None, metadata: 'dict[str, Any] | None' = None) -> 'LineageArtifact'` — Upsert one lineage artifact row.
- `lineage.record_audit_report(report, **kwargs)` — Record a lineage audit report in the durable catalog (0112).
- `lineage.record_edge(*, edge_type: 'str', from_artifact_id: 'str', to_artifact_id: 'str', execution_id: 'str | None' = None, edge_id: 'str | None' = None, metadata: 'dict[str, Any] | None' = None) -> 'LineageEdge'` — Upsert one directed upstream -> downstream artifact edge.
- `lineage.record_evidence_pack(pack, **kwargs)` — Record an evidence pack in the durable catalog (idempotent by digest).
- `lineage.record_execution(*, kind: 'str', execution_id: 'str | None' = None, name: 'str | None' = None, transform_id: 'str | None' = None, status: 'str | None' = None, params: 'dict[str, Any] | None' = None, params_json: 'str | None' = None, code_ref: 'str | None' = None, provider: 'str | None' = None, environment: 'dict[str, Any] | None' = None, environment_json: 'str | None' = None, input_artifact_ids: 'Iterable[str]' = (), output_artifact_ids: 'Iterable[str]' = (), input_table_versions: 'Iterable[dict[str, Any]]' = (), output_table_versions: 'Iterable[dict[str, Any]]' = (), started_at: 'datetime | None' = None, finished_at: 'datetime | None' = None, created_by: 'str | None' = None, metadata: 'dict[str, Any] | None' = None) -> 'LineageExecution'` — Upsert one execution row with input/output artifact pins.
- `lineage.record_external_context(**kwargs)` — Record one external-context row directly (idempotent by content digest).
- `lineage.record_rebuild_plan(plan, **kwargs)` — Record a rebuild plan in the durable catalog (idempotent by digest).
- `lineage.record_retention_policy(policy, **kwargs)` — Record a retention policy in the durable catalog (idempotent by digest).
- `lineage.refresh_graph(*, incremental: 'bool' = True, force_full: 'bool' = False, dry_run: 'bool' = False, reconcile: 'bool' = True) -> 'LineageRefreshReport'` — Project existing canonical tables into the lineage graph tables.
- `lineage.release_retention_policy(policy_id: 'str', **kwargs)` — Clear the holds a policy applied, leaving manual/other-policy holds.
- `lineage.replay_bundle(artifact: 'str', *, output_dir: 'str', kind: 'str | None' = None, checkpoint: 'bool' = False, where: 'str | None' = None, limit: 'int | None' = None, max_depth: 'int | None' = None, edge_types: 'Iterable[str]' = (), target_kinds: 'Iterable[str]' = (), refresh: 'bool' = True, include_mcap: 'bool' = True, include_video: 'bool' = False, include_gops: 'bool' = True, viewer_formats: 'Iterable[str]' = ('foxglove', 'rerun'), max_bytes: 'int | None' = None, max_files: 'int | None' = None, storage_options: 'Mapping[str, Any] | None' = None, auth_ref: 'str | None' = None)` — Export a replayable bundle (MCAP slices, video clip/GOP bytes) for an artifact.
- `lineage.require_hook_adapter(adapter: 'str') -> 'dict[str, str]'` — Validate that an optional lineage hook adapter package is installed.
- `lineage.require_integration_adapter(adapter: 'str') -> 'dict[str, str]'` — Validate that an optional external-system adapter package is installed.
- `lineage.resolve(handle: 'str', *, kind: 'str | None' = None, table_version: 'int | None' = None) -> 'LineageResolution'` — Resolve a handle to lineage roots with structured diagnostics (backlog 0102).
- `lineage.resolve_artifacts(handle: 'str', *, kind: 'str | None' = None, table_version: 'int | None' = None) -> 'tuple[str, ...]'` — Resolve a human artifact handle to one or more graph artifact ids.
- `lineage.resolve_external_urn(urn: 'str') -> 'str'` — Resolve an exported external URN back to a canonical artifact id.
- `lineage.resolve_retention_holds(**kwargs)` — Merge policy-applied + artifact-local holds into maintenance's pin shape.
- `lineage.retain(artifact: 'str', *, kind: 'str | None' = None, retain_until: 'datetime | str | None' = None, legal_hold: 'bool' = False, audit_hold: 'bool' = False, promotion_hold: 'bool' = False, owner: 'str | None' = None, reason: 'str | None' = None, refresh: 'bool' = True) -> 'LineageRetentionHold'` — Attach generic retention/audit-hold metadata to artifact handle(s).
- `lineage.retention_expiration_notices(**kwargs)` — Report holds whose retain_until has passed or is upcoming (append-safe notices).
- `lineage.retention_policies(**kwargs)` — List recorded retention policies newest-first, filtered and bounded.
- `lineage.retention_policy_events(**kwargs)` — Return retention-policy audit events, optionally filtered.
- `lineage.retry_lineage_delivery(backend: 'str', *, refresh: 'bool' = True, target: 'str | None' = None, endpoint_url: 'str | None' = None, auth_ref: 'str | None' = None, headers: 'Mapping[str, str] | None' = None, client: 'Any | None' = None, adapter: 'str | None' = None, producer: 'str | None' = None, created_by: 'str | None' = None)` — Retry OpenLineage or DataHub delivery, skipping delivered digests.
- `lineage.set_evidence_retention(digest: 'str', **kwargs)` — Update retention/protection metadata for a recorded pack.
- `lineage.set_external_context_retention(context_id: 'str', **kwargs)` — Update retention/protection/hold metadata for a recorded context.
- `lineage.trace(artifact: 'str', *, kind: 'str | None' = None, max_depth: 'int | None' = None, edge_types: 'Iterable[str]' = (), target_kinds: 'Iterable[str]' = (), created_after: 'datetime | str | None' = None, created_before: 'datetime | str | None' = None, table_versions: 'Mapping[str, int] | Iterable[str | tuple[str, int]]' = (), page_size: 'int | None' = None, page_token: 'str | None' = None) -> 'LineageGraph'` — Traverse upstream provenance for an artifact id or domain handle.
- `lineage.trace_checkpoint(model_run_id: 'str', *, where: 'str | None' = None, limit: 'int | None' = None) -> 'RegressionTrace'` — Trace a model/checkpoint run to a training snapshot and source rows.
- `lineage.update_rebuild_plan_status(plan_id: 'str', status: 'str', **kwargs)` — Move a recorded plan to a new lifecycle status (optimistically).
- `lineage.update_retention_policy_status(policy_id: 'str', status: 'str', **kwargs)` — Move a recorded policy to a new lifecycle status (optimistically).
- `lineage.worker_context(lineage_context: 'Any | None' = None, *, include_keys: 'Sequence[str] | None' = None, redact_keys: 'Sequence[str] | None' = None) -> 'dict[str, Any]'` — Return an explicit, redacted lineage context payload for workers.
- `lineage.worker_env(lineage_context: 'Any | None' = None, *, include_keys: 'Sequence[str] | None' = None, redact_keys: 'Sequence[str] | None' = None) -> 'dict[str, str]'` — Return env vars that explicitly propagate lineage context to workers.

## `lake.projections` — `LakeProjections`

Convenience namespace exposed as ``lake.projections``.

- `projections.dataset(format: 'str', snapshot_name: 'str', *, mode: 'str | ProjectionMode' = <ProjectionMode.LIVE: 'live'>, require_native: 'bool' = False, shard_size: 'int' = 1000, compression: 'str' = 'none', lineage_context: 'Any | None' = None, **training_options: 'Any') -> 'ProjectionLiveDataset | ProjectionManifest'`
- `projections.export(format: 'str', snapshot_name: 'str', *, out: 'str | Path', require_native: 'bool' = False, shard_size: 'int' = 1000, compression: 'str' = 'none', lineage_context: 'Any | None' = None) -> 'ProjectionManifest'`
- `projections.format(name: 'str') -> 'LakeProjectionBinding'`
- `projections.materialization_summary(snapshot_name: 'str', *, formats: 'Sequence[str] | None' = None, require_native: 'bool' = False, shard_size: 'int' = 1000, compression: 'str' = 'none', lineage_context: 'Any | None' = None) -> 'dict[str, Any]'`
- `projections.plan(format: 'str', snapshot_name: 'str', *, require_native: 'bool' = False, shard_size: 'int' = 1000, compression: 'str' = 'none', lineage_context: 'Any | None' = None) -> 'ProjectionManifest'`

## `lake.tracker` — `LakeTrackerSync`

Convenience namespace exposed as ``lake.tracker``.

- `tracker.drift(bundle: 'Mapping[str, Any]', **kwargs: 'Any') -> 'TrackerDriftReport'` — Report which external runs conflict with canonical rows (read-only).
- `tracker.export_bundle(**kwargs: 'Any') -> 'TrackerExportReport'` — Export canonical manifests into a portable, round-trippable bundle.
- `tracker.import_bundle(bundle: 'Mapping[str, Any] | None' = None, **kwargs: 'Any') -> 'TrackerImportReport'` — Import an external manifest bundle into the canonical manifest tables.
- `tracker.require_adapter(source: 'str') -> 'dict[str, Any]'` — Validate a tracker adapter is installed (raises an install hint if not).

## `lake.training` — `LakeTraining`

Convenience namespace exposed as ``lake.training``.

- `training.aggregate_reports(reports: Any, *, job_id: str | None = None)` — Combine per-worker loader reports into one job-level report (backlog 0123).
- `training.aligned_dataset(alignment: str | None = None, *, alignment_id: str | None = None, name: str | None = None, streams: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | None = None, columns: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | None = None, statuses: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | str | None = None, require_streams: bool | collections.abc.Sequence[str] = False, allow_missing: bool = True, min_confidence: float | None = None, shuffle: bool = False, shuffle_seed: int | None = 0, epoch: int = 0, worker_id: int = 0, num_workers: int = 1, resume_from: int = 0, features: str | bool = 'metadata', decoder: str = 'auto', feature_cache: str = 'none', feature_cache_size: int = 128, backend: str = 'auto', cache_policy: str = 'none', prewarm: bool = False, prewarm_options: collections.abc.Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, allow_fallback: bool = False, fallback: str | bool | None = None) -> lancedb_robotics.training.AlignedFrameTrainingDataset`
- `training.attach_external_refs(training_run_id: str, refs: collections.abc.Mapping[str, typing.Any], *, replace: bool = False)` — Attach MLflow/W&B/etc. references to an existing training manifest.
- `training.attach_model_external_refs(model_artifact_id: str, refs: collections.abc.Mapping[str, typing.Any], *, replace: bool = False)` — Attach external tracker/artifact references to an existing model row.
- `training.backfill_aligned_ticks(alignment: str | None = None, *, alignment_id: str | None = None, name: str | None = None, batch_size: int = 4, replace: bool = True, verify: bool = True) -> dict[str, typing.Any]`
- `training.cancel_prewarm_job(prewarm_id: str, *, reason: str | None = None) -> dict[str, typing.Any]` — Cancel a no-longer-needed prewarm JobRun; complete JobRuns are left as-is.
- `training.checkpoints(**kwargs: Any)` — Bounded/indexed model-artifact query (by training run, alias, checksum...).
- `training.conformance_matrix(*, include_local_endpoint: bool = False)` — Return the Enterprise-training compatibility matrix (backlog 0116).
- `training.dataset(snapshot_name: str, *, columns: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | None = None, filters: collections.abc.Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, shuffle: bool = False, shuffle_seed: int | None = 0, epoch: int = 0, worker_id: int = 0, num_workers: int = 1, resume_from: int = 0, time_windows: collections.abc.Mapping[str, collections.abc.Sequence[float]] | None = None, media: str | bool = 'metadata', payloads: str | bool | None = None, decoder: str = 'auto', media_cache: str = 'none', media_cache_size: int = 128, backend: str = 'auto', cache_policy: str = 'none', prewarm: bool = False, prewarm_options: collections.abc.Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, allow_fallback: bool = False, fallback: str | bool | None = None) -> lancedb_robotics.training.LanceTrainingDataset`
- `training.epoch_backend_capability() -> dict[str, typing.Any]` — Report available epoch-order execution backends for this lake.
- `training.expire_checkpoint(model_artifact_id: str, *, force: bool = False)` — Delete a checkpoint/model artifact (refused if protected unless ``force``).
- `training.expire_prewarm_jobs() -> list[dict[str, typing.Any]]` — Sweep and mark TTL-elapsed prewarm JobRuns as ``expired`` (maintenance).
- `training.expire_report(report_id: str, *, force: bool = False)` — Delete a persisted training report (refused if protected unless ``force``).
- `training.expire_run(training_run_id: str, *, force: bool = False)` — Delete a training run (refused if protected unless ``force``).
- `training.get_report(report_id: str | None = None, **kwargs: Any)` — Reload the full backend report for a report id or run/epoch/worker.
- `training.index_aligned_predicates(*, include_frames: bool = True, refresh: bool = False) -> tuple[dict[str, typing.Any], ...]` — Build scalar indexes for aligned training hot predicates.
- `training.list_checkpoints(**kwargs: Any)` — Deterministic paged model-artifact listing with continuation tokens.
- `training.list_runs(**kwargs: Any)` — Deterministic paged training-run listing with continuation tokens.
- `training.page_cache_prewarm_plan(snapshot_name: str, *, columns: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | None = None, filters: collections.abc.Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, shuffle: bool = False, shuffle_seed: int | None = 0, epoch: int = 0, backend: str = 'enterprise', cache_policy: str = 'epoch', include_heavy: bool = False, allow_fallback: bool = False, fallback: str | bool | None = None, concurrency: int | None = None, estimate: bool = True) -> dict[str, typing.Any]` — Build the Enterprise query-node page-cache prewarm plan for a snapshot (0122).
- `training.permutation_capability() -> dict[str, typing.Any]` — Report whether the native LanceDB ``Permutation`` reader can back a plan.
- `training.permutation_plan(snapshot_name: str, *, columns: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | None = None, filters: collections.abc.Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, shuffle: bool = True, shuffle_seed: int | None = 0, epoch: int = 0, worker_id: int = 0, num_workers: int = 1, resume_from: int = 0, time_windows: collections.abc.Mapping[str, collections.abc.Sequence[float]] | None = None, output_format: str = 'arrow', verify: bool = True) -> Any` — Back a version-pinned snapshot plan with the native ``Permutation`` reader.
- `training.plan_executor_conformance(snapshot: str, *, include_local_endpoint: bool = False, strict: bool = False)` — Prove the live query-node read-client contract for ``snapshot`` (0119).
- `training.prewarm_job(prewarm_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None` — Return a single durable prewarm JobRun by id (with full status history).
- `training.prewarm_job_status(prewarm_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None` — Return the 0072-shaped status envelope for a durable prewarm JobRun.
- `training.prewarm_jobs(*, status: str | None = None, policy: str | None = None, limit: int | None = None) -> list[dict[str, typing.Any]]` — List durable Enterprise cache-prewarm JobRuns for this lake (backlog 0121).
- `training.query_node_conformance(snapshot: str, *, include_local_endpoint: bool = False, strict: bool = False)` — Prove the live query-node read-client contract for ``snapshot`` (0119).
- `training.query_reports(**kwargs: Any)` — Bounded training-report query with an execution plan (by run/backend/fallback).
- `training.query_warm_plan(snapshot_name: str, *, columns: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | None = None, filters: collections.abc.Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, shuffle: bool = False, shuffle_seed: int | None = 0, epoch: int = 0, chunk_size: int = 512, include_heavy: bool = False, backend: str = 'auto', allow_fallback: bool = False, fallback: str | bool | None = None) -> dict[str, typing.Any]` — Build a query-driven cache-warm plan for a snapshot (backlog 0348).
- `training.record_checkpoint(**kwargs: Any)` — Record a checkpoint/model artifact produced by a training run.
- `training.record_report(report: Any | None = None, *, dataset: Any | None = None, **kwargs: Any)` — Persist an Enterprise training loader/backend report (backlog 0115).
- `training.record_run(snapshot: str | None = None, **kwargs: Any)` — Record a training run manifest for a pinned dataset snapshot.
- `training.report_metrics(**kwargs: Any)` — Sum cache hits/misses, bytes read, and PE fanout across matched reports.
- `training.report_retention_plan(**kwargs: Any)` — Report which persisted training reports are protected vs safe to expire.
- `training.reports(**kwargs: Any)` — Deterministic paged listing of persisted training-report history.
- `training.retention_plan(**kwargs: Any)` — Report which training/eval manifests are protected vs safe to expire.
- `training.retry_prewarm_job(prewarm_id: str) -> dict[str, typing.Any]` — Re-submit a failed/canceled/expired prewarm JobRun (increments retry count).
- `training.row_plan_page(handle: collections.abc.Mapping[str, typing.Any], *, worker_id: int = 0, num_workers: int = 1, resume_from: int = 0, page_token: str | None = None) -> dict[str, typing.Any]` — Fetch one bounded page of a server-side row-plan handle.
- `training.row_plan_pages(handle: collections.abc.Mapping[str, typing.Any], *, worker_id: int = 0, num_workers: int = 1, resume_from: int = 0) -> list[dict[str, typing.Any]]` — Return all of a worker's bounded pages for a server-side row-plan handle.
- `training.run_conformance(snapshot: str, *, include_local_endpoint: bool = False, strict: bool = False, **kwargs: Any)` — Replay ``snapshot`` through every backend case and injected fault.
- `training.runs(**kwargs: Any)` — Bounded/indexed training-run query with an execution plan (backlog 0100).
- `training.server_side_row_plan(snapshot_name: str, *, columns: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | None = None, filters: collections.abc.Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, shuffle: bool = False, shuffle_seed: int | None = 0, epoch: int = 0, backend: str = 'enterprise', cache_policy: str = 'none', allow_fallback: bool = False, fallback: str | bool | None = None, page_size: int = 1024, store: str = 'auto') -> dict[str, typing.Any]` — Build a version-pinned server-side row-plan artifact and return its handle.
- `training.validate_report(report: Any) -> lancedb_robotics.training_report_schema.ReportValidation` — Validate a loader report against the ``.../v1`` schema + redaction contract (0124).

## `lake.video` — `LakeVideo`

Convenience namespace exposed as ``lake.video``.

- `video.conform_source(samples: 'Iterable[Mapping[str, Any]]' = (), *, decoder: 'str' = 'auto', fail_on_mismatch: 'bool' = False, created_by: 'str' = 'lancedb-robotics') -> 'VideoConformanceReport'`
- `video.encode(*, video_id: 'str | None' = None, episode_id: 'str | None' = None, camera_key: 'str | None' = None, codec: 'str' = 'lrb-gop-zlib', gop_size: 'int' = 2, resolution: 'str | None' = None, fps: 'float | None' = None, nvdec_compatible: 'bool' = False, created_by: 'str' = 'lancedb-robotics') -> 'VideoEncodingReport'`
- `video.encodings(*, video_id: 'str | None' = None) -> 'tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]'`
- `video.seek(episode: 'str | int | Any', frame: 'int', *, camera_key: 'str | None' = None, encoding_id: 'str | None' = None, decoder: 'str' = 'auto') -> 'VideoFrame'`
